description: Default per-fuel CO2 emission factors and net calorific values for stationary combustion in the energy industries. Consumed by `plp2gtopt --emissions-file` to fill missing emission_factors on Fuel elements that the PLP source / PLEXOS overlay did not populate. PLEXOS-shipped factors always take precedence; this file only fills gaps. Override with `--emissions-file PATH` pointing at a user-curated JSON of the same shape, or disable with `--no-emissions-file`.
source: IPCC Guidelines for National Greenhouse Gas Inventories, 2006 — Volume 2 (Energy), Chapter 1 (Introduction), Table 1.4 (Default CO2 Emission Factors for Stationary Combustion in the Energy Industries) and Chapter 1, Table 1.2 (Default Net Calorific Values). Available at https://www.ipcc-nggip.iges.or.jp/public/2006gl/vol2.html.

units.co2_combustion: tCO2 / GJ — multiplies `Generator.heat_rate` (GJ / MWh) at LP build to recover per-MWh tCO2 (gtopt convention).
units.co2_upstream: tCO2 / GJ — fuel-cycle upstream factor (extraction / processing / transport). IPCC stationary-combustion default = 0; populate from a fuel-cycle LCA when available.
units.heat_content: GJ per fuel-unit (net calorific value, NCV / LHV). Carried through to `Fuel.heat_content` for downstream tooling; gtopt does not consume it directly when factors are already in tCO2/GJ.
units.ch4_combustion: tCH4 / GJ — IPCC 2006 Vol 2 Ch 2 Table 2.2 stationary-combustion default. Mass-basis, NOT CO2-equivalent (no GWP weighting); EmissionZone basket applies weights if needed.
units.ch4_upstream: tCH4 / GJ — fuel-cycle upstream methane (extraction/processing leakage). IPCC stationary-combustion default = 0; LNG and gas transport can add substantial values from a project-specific LCA.
units.n2o_combustion: tN2O / GJ — IPCC 2006 Vol 2 Ch 2 Table 2.2 stationary-combustion default. Mass-basis.
units.n2o_upstream: tN2O / GJ — fuel-cycle upstream N2O. IPCC stationary-combustion default = 0.

notes: All factors are net-calorific-value (NCV / LHV) basis, matching the IPCC default.
co2_combustion values are the IPCC 2006 default — NOT the 95% confidence-interval lower/upper bounds. For uncertainty propagation refer to Table 1.4 in the source document.

Biomass: IPCC default 112000 kg CO2/TJ is reported here as `co2_combustion_gross` for users running a gross-carbon-pricing scenario. The default `co2_combustion = 0` follows IPCC AFOLU-sector biogenic-zero accounting (combustion CO2 is offset by sustainable biomass uptake).

Natural gas vs LNG: combustion factor is identical (the fuel at the burner tip is the same gas). Liquefaction / transport / re-gasification add an upstream factor that varies by source; not in the IPCC stationary-combustion default.
Geothermal: stationary-combustion default = 0 (non-combustion). Non-condensable-gas (NCG) releases at individual fields can be material but are site-specific and not part of an IPCC default.
Aliases are normalized identically to fuel names: case folded, all of space / underscore / dash stripped. So `gas-natural`, `Gas Natural`, `GAS_NATURAL` all match the same entry.

CH4 / N2O factors are ~10^5 smaller than CO2 — kept in the same t/GJ mass basis (no GWP weighting at the factor level). Apply GWP weights via the EmissionZone basket if running a CO2-equivalent scenario (CH4=28, N2O=265 per IPCC AR5 GWP-100).
Biomass / biogas CH4 + N2O are NOT biogenic-zero (unlike CO2): they are combustion byproducts. The default carries non-zero values per IPCC Table 2.2.

fuels.name: diesel
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Gas/Diesel Oil (74100 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 43.0 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Gas/Diesel Oil (3 kg CH4/TJ, 0.6 kg N2O/TJ)

fuels.name: fuel_oil
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Residual Fuel Oil (77400 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 40.4 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Residual Fuel Oil (3, 0.6)

fuels.name: natural_gas
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Natural Gas (56100 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 48.0 TJ/Gg. The plain 'gas' alias catches CEN-Chile fuel names of the form Gas_<plant>_<band> (LNG and pipeline NG share the same IPCC combustion factor 0.0561 tCO2/GJ — distinction only matters for upstream LCA factor).; Table 2.2 — Natural Gas (1, 0.1)

fuels.name: lng
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Natural Gas at the burner tip (56100 kg CO2/TJ). Upstream LNG-chain factor (liquefaction + tanker + regas) varies 6–15 kg CO2/GJ by route and is NOT in the IPCC stationary-combustion default — populate co2_upstream from a project-specific LCA when carbon accounting matters.; Table 2.2 — combustion same as Natural Gas (1, 0.1)

fuels.name: coal_bituminous
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Other Bituminous Coal (94600 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 25.8 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Other Bituminous Coal (1, 1.5)

fuels.name: coal_sub_bituminous
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Sub-Bituminous Coal (96100 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 18.9 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Sub-Bituminous Coal (1, 1.5)

fuels.name: coal_anthracite
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Anthracite (98300 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 26.7 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Anthracite (1, 1.5)

fuels.name: coal_lignite
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Lignite (101000 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 11.9 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Lignite (1, 1.5)

fuels.name: coking_coal
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Coking Coal (94600 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 28.2 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Coking Coal (1, 1.5)

fuels.name: petcoke
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Petroleum Coke (97500 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 32.5 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Petroleum Coke (3, 0.6 — defaults to liquid-fuel row)

fuels.name: lpg
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Liquefied Petroleum Gases (63100 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 47.3 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — LPG (1, 0.1)

fuels.name: kerosene
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Other Kerosene (71900 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 43.8 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Other Kerosene (3, 0.6)

fuels.name: naphtha
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Naphtha (73300 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 44.5 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Naphtha (3, 0.6 — defaults to liquid-fuel row)

fuels.name: crude_oil
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Crude Oil (73300 kg CO2/TJ); Table 1.2 NCV 42.3 TJ/Gg; Table 2.2 — Crude Oil (3, 0.6)

fuels.name: biomass
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Wood/Wood Waste (112000 kg CO2/TJ gross); biogenic-zero default per AFOLU-sector accounting (combustion CO2 offset by sustainable uptake). Use co2_combustion_gross for a non-zero biomass scenario.; Table 2.2 — Wood/Wood Waste (30 kg CH4/TJ, 4 kg N2O/TJ — NOT biogenic-zero like CO2: combustion byproducts)

fuels.name: biogas
fuels.ipcc_reference: Table 1.4 — Other Biogas (54600 kg CO2/TJ gross). Biogenic-zero default; use co2_combustion_gross when biogenic CO2 must be priced.; Table 2.2 — Other Biogas (1, 0.1)

fuels.name: geothermal
fuels.ipcc_reference: Geothermal is non-combustion. IPCC stationary-combustion default = 0. Non-condensable-gas releases at specific fields are site-specific and not part of an IPCC default.; Geothermal: non-combustion (0, 0)
